evals.prompt: check for sensitive info in evals/fixtures/messy-project
evals.expected_output: Flags the live-looking secrets, the .env, the customer PII (names, emails, mobiles, NI/NHS numbers, cards), and GDPR health data; skips node_modules; ignores placeholders; gives rotate/gitignore advice.
expectations: Flags the OpenAI API key as a secret
Flags the Stripe live key as a secret
Identifies the AWS access key id
Flags the Google API key
Flags the RSA private key block
Flags the database credentials embedded in the connection string
Flags all three UK National Insurance numbers
Flags the NHS number in notes.md
Flags the payment card numbers
Flags customer emails and UK mobile numbers as PII
Flags the mental-health note as GDPR special-category data
Catches the free-text 'diabetes medication' health note in customers.csv
Does not treat the node_modules example key as a real finding
Does not report 'changeme' / 'your-sentry-dsn-here' as real secrets
Recommends rotating exposed secrets and removing/gitignoring .env + customer data

evals.prompt: I'm about to push evals/fixtures/clean-project to a public GitHub repo. Anything personal or any secrets in here I should worry about before I do?
evals.expected_output: Reports the project as essentially safe to push, without crying wolf over the env-var key, the placeholder, or the example.com email.
expectations: Concludes the folder is safe to push (no real secrets or PII)
Does not flag the env-var key read (os.environ) as a leaked secret
Does not flag the <your-api-key-here> placeholder as a real secret
Treats noreply@example.com as a non-issue, not a real concern
Produces no false CRITICAL/HIGH findings

evals.prompt: can you look through evals/fixtures/prose-pii and tell me if there's anything sensitive or personal in there?
evals.expected_output: Catches the free-text PII the regexes can't: names tied to DOBs and addresses, plus GDPR special-category health and religious data; treats it as high-sensitivity.
expectations: Identifies all three individuals' full names
Identifies dates of birth
Identifies home / postal addresses
Flags health special-category data (cancer/chemotherapy and depression)
Flags religious belief as special-category data
Characterises it as high-sensitivity / GDPR special-category data needing careful handling
